top_signals.title: Yen Intervention Risk Rises After Katayama-Bessent Talks
top_signals.summary: Currency traders are on high alert for intervention after further weakness in the yen and a call between Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama and US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Nvidia seeks to make humanoid AI robots safer around humans
top_signals.summary: The chipmaker is offering software and semiconductors that will allow humanoids to truly interact with people — even making physical contact if necessary.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Federal Reserve Board requests comment on proposal to require certain payment stablecoin issuers to maintain an effective customer identification program
top_signals.summary: Federal Reserve Board requests comment on proposal to require certain payment stablecoin issuers to maintain an effective customer identification program
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Bank of England launches policy statement and draft rules on regulating systemic stablecoins
top_signals.summary: The Bank of England has today published its policy statement and draft Code of Practice (rules) for systemic stablecoin issuers.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with former employee of Bank of Eufaula and S N B Bancshares, Inc.
top_signals.summary: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with former employee of Bank of Eufaula and S N B Bancshares, Inc.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Iceland kills first whales since 2023, resuming commercial hunt
top_signals.summary: Iceland is one of only three countries that still openly permit whaling, alongside Norway and Japan.
